alt: Want to feel old? Wait.

title: Questions for God
alt: What sins could possibly darken the heart of a STEAMBOAT? I asked The Shadow, but he says he only covers men.

title: Turnabout
alt: Whenever I miss a shot with a sci-fi weapon, I say 'Apollo retroreflector' really fast, just in case.

title: Paleontology Museum
alt: Nowadays the only ones left that do anything are turtles.

title: Coronavirus Research
alt: "Also, reading 500 coronavirus papers in a row and not sleeping? Probably not great for you either, but I haven't found any studies confirming that yet. I'll keep looking."

title: Rejection
alt: Perhaps you need a crash course in taking hints. Here's your first lesson: We're not actually walking somewhere together; I'm trying to leave this conversation and you're following me.

title: Stargazing
alt: Some of you may be thinking, 'But wait, isn't the brightest star in our sky the Sun?' I think that's a great question and you should totally ask it. On the infinite tree of possible conversations spread out before us, I think that's definitely the most promising branch.

title: Irony Definition
alt: Can you stop glaring at me like that? It makes me feel really ironic.

title: Joshing
alt: You'll be moved up from 49 of ~7 billion to 31 of ~7 billion.

title: Attention Span
alt: I didn't even realize they MADE a novelization of "Surf Ninjas." How did you-- Oh my god, it's signed by the author?!

title: Board Game Argument: Legacy
alt: Listen, you need to get over your reluctance to permanently alter a game. Now roll 2d6 to determine how many ounces of soda to spill into the box.

title: Dear Diary
alt: Dear Diary: UNSUBSCRIBE

title: Lightsabers
alt: A lot of Jedi romances start with this turning into a Lady and the Tramp spaghetti situation.

title: Tech Loops
alt: And when I think about it, a lot of "things I want to do" are just learning about and discussing new tools for tinkering with the chain.

title: Journal 3
alt: Oh, and, uh, if the Russian government asks, that submarine was always there.

title: Parity Conservation
alt: Bloody Mary is made of antimatter. It explains so much.

title: Helping
alt: Oh, look, the 'make everything better' button was here behind the bookshelf all along.

title: Superzoom
alt: *click* Let him know he's got a stain on his shirt, though.

title: Drinking Fountains
alt: I've always wondered whether you could drink slowly enough, and eliminate fast enough, that you just sort of peed continuously. But I'm afraid to try because I worry someone might call while I'm doing it and ask what I'm up to, and I won't be able to think of a lie.

title: Logic Gates
alt: In C, the multiocular O represents the bitwise norxondor gorgonax.

title: Server Problem
alt: Protip: Annoy Ray Kurzweil by always referring to it as the 'Cybersingularity'.

title: Pet Peeve #114
alt: I'm reading a goddamn book, thank you very much.

title: Schwarzschild's Cat
alt: Cats can be smaller than the critical limit, but they're unobservable. If one shrinks enough that it crosses the limit, it just appears to get cuter and cuter as it slowly fades from view.

title: Hard Reboot
alt: Googling inevitably reveals that my problem is caused by a known bug triggered by doing [the exact combination of things I want to do]. I can fix it, or wait a few years until I don't want that combination of things anymore, using the kitchen timer until then.

title: Rainbow
alt: Listen, in a few thousand years you'll invent a game called 'SimCity' which has a 'disaster' button, and then you'll understand.
